perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in all respects in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without giving effect to any conflicts of laws principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in all respects in accordance with the laws of mainland China, without giving effect to any conflicts of laws principles.
perturbation.explanation: The governing law clause has been changed from New York to mainland China. This could create enforceability issues if the contract is primarily executed or has significant connections to the United States. Furthermore, it may introduce contradictions if specific clauses rely on interpretations under US law, particularly regarding intellectual property, indemnification, or dispute resolution.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Choice of Law Principles Under US Contract Law
perturbation.law_explanation: US contract law respects choice-of-law provisions unless the chosen state has no substantial relationship to the parties or the transaction, or application of the chosen law would be contrary to a fundamental policy of a state which has a materially greater interest than the chosen state. By stipulating PRC law, while the Licensee is a US company based in New York, the agreement could be challenged if significant aspects of the performance occur in the US, conflicting with established US legal precedents about contracts performed largely within the US.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: conflict of laws | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you conflict of laws Conflict of laws refers to a difference between the laws of two or more jurisdictions with some connection to a case, such that the outcome depends on which jurisdiction's law will be used to resolve each issue in dispute . The conflicting legal rules may come from U.S. federal law, the laws of U.S. states, or the laws of other countries. The process by which a court determines what law to apply is sometimes referred to as "characterization" or "classification." This determination must be made in accordance with the law of the forum . A federal court in a case before it based on diversity of citizenship , for example, determines the conflict of law issue as if it were the highest court in the state in which it is sitting. Courts faced with a choice of law issue generally have two choices: A court can apply the law of the forum ( lex fori ) -- which is usually the result when the question of what law to apply is procedural . Or the court can apply the law of the site of the transaction or occurrence that gave rise to the litigation in the first place ( lex loci ) -- this is usually the controlling law selected when the matter is substantive . Federal courts play by different rules than state courts because federal jurisdiction is limited to what has been enumerated in the Constitution . The rules that federal courts must obey regarding which laws to apply are extremely complex. perturbation.justification: The original text specifies New York law, whereas the changed text specifies mainland China law. The Law Explanation states that choice-of-law provisions are respected unless the chosen state has no substantial relationship, or its application would contradict a fundamental policy of a state with a materially greater interest. If the Licensee is a US company in New York and significant performance occurs in the US, applying PRC law could be challenged, potentially contradicting US legal precedents. Scraped Law Snippet 1 discusses conflict of laws, noting that a court determines what law to apply, and federal courts have specific rules regarding which laws to apply. Changing the governing law to mainland China creates a structural flaw that could result in a legal contradiction if it undermines established US legal precedents for contracts performed largely within the US, as explained in the Law Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute, controversy and/or difference which may arise out of or in connection with or in relation to this Agreement, shall be solely and finally settled by binding arbitration pursuant to then-current rules of the International Chamber of Commerce. Such arbitration shall be held in New York, New York.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute, controversy and/or difference which may arise out of or in connection with or in relation to this Agreement, shall be solely and finally settled by informal negotiation between the parties. If negotiation fails, the parties may pursue any legal remedy available to them.
perturbation.explanation: The mandatory arbitration clause has been replaced with a clause allowing for informal negotiation and subsequent litigation. This removes the requirement for binding arbitration and opens the door to potential lawsuits, contradicting the original intent of resolving disputes through arbitration.
perturbation.law_explanation: The FAA favors and enforces arbitration agreements. By removing the arbitration clause, the modified agreement directly contradicts the FAA's policy of upholding private agreements to arbitrate disputes, potentially leading to increased litigation costs and delays that the FAA seeks to avoid.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate U.S. Code Notes prev | next A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract or as otherwise provided in chapter 4. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670 ; Pub. L. 117–90, § 2(b)(1)(A) , Mar. 3, 2022 , 136 Stat. 27 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 2, 43 Stat. 883 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2022— Pub. L. 117–90 inserted “or as otherwise provided in chapter 4” before period at end. Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Effective Date of 2022 Amendment Amendment by Pub. L. 117–90 applicable with respect to any dispute or claim that arises or accrues on or after Mar. 3, 2022 , see section 3 of Pub. L. 117–90 , set out as an Effective Date note under section 401 of this title . U.S. Code Toolbox Law about... perturbation.justification: The modified contract text contradicts the FAA's policy favoring arbitration agreements, as supported by 9 U.S. Code § 2, which states that agreements to arbitrate are "valid, irrevocable, and enforceable." By removing the mandatory arbitration clause and allowing for litigation, the changed text directly undermines this legal principle, contradicting the law's intent to uphold private arbitration agreements and potentially increasing litigation.

perturbation.original_text: THE PARTIES SPECIFICALLY WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY COURT WITH RESPECT TO ANY CONTRACTUAL, TORTIOUS, OR STATUTORY CLAIM, COUNTERCLAIM, OR CROSS-CLAIM AGAINST THE OTHER ARISING OUT OF OR CONNECTED IN ANY WAY TO THIS AGREEMENT, BECAUSE THE PARTIES HERETO, BOTH OF WHOM ARE REPRESENTED BY COUNSEL, BELIEVE THAT THE COMPLEX COMMERCIAL AND PROFESSIONAL ASPECTS OF THEIR DEALINGS WITH ONE ANOTHER MAKE A JURY DETERMINATION NEITHER DESIRABLE NOR APPROPRIATE.
perturbation.changed_text: THE PARTIES AGREE THAT ANY DISPUTES ARISING UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL BE RESOLVED IN A COURT OF LAW, AND EACH PARTY RETAINS ALL RIGHTS TO A TRIAL BY JURY AS PROVIDED BY LAW.
perturbation.explanation: The original jury trial waiver has been replaced with a clause explicitly preserving the right to a jury trial. This directly contradicts the original intent of waiving the right to a jury trial and introduces ambiguity about the parties' agreed-upon dispute resolution process. It also removes the justification that the complexity of the matter renders jury determination inappropriate.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Seventh Amendment to the United States Constitution
perturbation.law_explanation: While the Seventh Amendment guarantees the right to a jury trial in civil cases exceeding a certain value, it also allows for the knowing and voluntary waiver of that right. By reversing the waiver and explicitly preserving the right, the modified clause contradicts the principle of contractual freedom to waive constitutional rights and introduces uncertainty as to whether parties truly intended to waive this right by including the waiver initially.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. Wex Resources Criminal Law Criminal Procedure Jury Other Resources US Code: Title 18 Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure ‹ Sixth Amendment up Eighth Amendment › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Service
perturbation.justification: The original text waives the right to a jury trial, while the changed text explicitly preserves it. This creates a direct contradiction. The Seventh Amendment, as stated in Scraped Law Snippet 2, preserves the right to a jury trial, absent waiver by the parties. The modified text reverses this waiver, directly contradicting the original agreement and introducing a structural flaw as the parties are actively disagreeing on if they should waive their right to a jury trial. This contradicts the principle of contractual freedom.
